pi_items.source: Verified via setup_task.sh which explicitly restores the Muncie project to this directory.
Verified via install_hec_ras.sh and task_utils.sh which run HEC-RAS to produce this output file, and by downloading/extracting the US Army Corps HEC-RAS Muncie dataset.
Verified from the task.json description which explicitly instructs the agent to save the output here.

Verified from task description and confirmed via independent code analysis of the HDF output file: 44 of 61 cross-sections cross the 8.0ft depth threshold during simulation, making the parameter meaningful.
Verified from the task.json description which strictly mandates these specific column headers.

privileged_info_summary: The Muncie dataset is a real-world HEC-RAS 6.6 project modeling the White River at Muncie, Indiana. Code execution and analysis of the HEC-RAS unsteady flow results (`Muncie.p04.tmp.hdf`) independently confirms that out of 61 total cross sections, 17 begin the simulation with a channel depth greater than 8.0 feet (at t=0), while the remaining 44 exceed 8.0 feet later during the run, validating that the 8.0 ft threshold provides a dynamic and mathematically sound benchmark for computing flood arrival times.
